pm_status_summary.summary: Wave 7 DONE. Agent 03 diagnosed and fixed WK58-BUG-004 (invisible instanced trees). ROOT CAUSE: two Panda3D-level subtleties. (1) Bounding-volume bug: instanced GeomNodes had MODEL-LOCAL bsphere (e.g. center=(0,0.6,0) r=0.7), but per-instance world positions are computed in the shader from a buffer texture. Panda3D's view-frustum cull saw the local-space bsphere as out-of-view and dropped the ENTIRE instanced draw every frame even though set_instance_count(N)>0 and the buffer texture had valid world positions. (2) Material auto-bind bug: custom GLSL fragment shader read p3d_Material.baseColor, but Panda3D only auto-binds p3d_Material via the auto-shader generator — custom shaders see uninitialized struct values (cyan), so trees rendered as cyan teardrops even after bounds fix. FIX: (a) OmniBoundingVolume() + set_final(True) on each per-model NodePath so cull always passes; (b) _bake_per_geom_material_to_vertex_colors() mutates loaded GeomNode at load time, adding vertex-color column populated per-Geom from MaterialAttrib (handles Kenney multi-Geom layouts: trunk+foliage+crown); (c) GLSL shader rewritten to read p3d_Color (baked) and pass to fragment. Env var KINGDOM_URSINA_INSTANCED_TREES default stays '1'. PM confirmed via screenshot inspection: trees visible at full forest density. Agent 03 added regression test TestInstancedTreeVisibility::test_instanced_geom_uses_omni_bounds_post_setup (loads real Kenney GLB offscreen, asserts active_count>=1 and bounds string contains 'infinite'). Pytest 6/6 PASS. qa_smoke PASS in both env modes. Trees honored no-density-cut directive: all 2,083 trees register.

wave7_perf_post_fix.explanation: Slight FPS regression vs Wave 6 (31.0 -> 28.4) is the cost of correctly rendering trees: OmniBoundingVolume() disables per-GeomNode view-frustum cull on the 16 instanced models (CPU chunk filter still trims per-instance draws to ~341 at default cam, so GPU vertex hit is modest), plus load-time vertex-color bake. Acceptable tradeoff: trees are back at full forest density, baseline is still +14.9 FPS (2.1x improvement).

pm_status_summary.summary: Wave 8 ran 4.C GeoMipTerrain LOD as a flag-gated dual path. Jaimie playtested with KINGDOM_URSINA_GEOMIPTERRAIN='1' on 2026-05-19 and reported NO measurable zoom-out FPS improvement plus a visible elevation regression (heightfield 250 -> 513 bilinear resample distorts heights at distance). Decision: stay on env='0' (custom Mesh path, the existing WK53 implementation). The GeoMipTerrain code remains in the repo behind the env flag for future research but is NOT the default and should not be re-proposed without a different heightfield strategy. See memory project_geomipterrain_lod_negative_result.md. Sprint perf wins from W2/W3/W4/W6/W7 are unaffected and stand: 13.5 -> 28.4 FPS post-/revealmap (W7 final), +14.9 FPS / 2.1x improvement over the pre-WK58 baseline. Wave 8 outcome: negative result correctly identified and documented; no further action on this path.

wave8_jaimie_verdict.decision: Leave KINGDOM_URSINA_GEOMIPTERRAIN default '0' (custom Mesh). Code stays in repo behind flag, dormant.

wave8_what_we_learned: The zoom-out FPS pain Jaimie originally felt post-W6 may have been less severe than estimated (W7 trees-back + GeoMipTerrain-off is apparently 'a good level'); the actual remaining GPU cost is elsewhere.
Heightfield resampling to fit GeoMipTerrain's 2^n+1 dimension expectation drifts elevation enough to be visually noticeable at distance.
Future GPU-side work should target 4.B (simpler fog fragment shader) rather than 4.C unless the heightfield resolution strategy changes.
